description: Mosaic-era HTML compatibility-floor cases for the current generated Rust wrapper.

cases.description: Duplicate attributes keep the first value and report duplicate-attribute
Unexpected characters in unquoted attribute values are preserved with parse errors
cases.input: <a data=one=two sq=x'y lt=x<y eq=x=y tick=x`y dq=x"y>
tokens: StartTag(name=a, attributes=[data=one=two, sq=x'y, lt=x<y, eq=x=y, tick=x`y, dq=x"y], self_closing=false)

cases.description: NULL characters in data and attribute values are replaced with U+FFFD
NULL characters in tag and attribute names are replaced with U+FFFD
NULL characters in comments and bogus comments are replaced with U+FFFD
NULL characters in DOCTYPE names and identifiers are replaced with U+FFFD
DOCTYPE without a name emits a force-quirks doctype
DOCTYPE with only whitespace before > emits a force-quirks doctype
DOCTYPE name cut off by EOF emits the current name and marks force-quirks
DOCTYPE keyword cut off by EOF emits a force-quirks doctype
Malformed DOCTYPE keyword text marks force-quirks
DOCTYPE after-name state cut off by EOF marks force-quirks
Mosaic-era PUBLIC doctypes preserve their public identifier
DOCTYPE PUBLIC declarations preserve both public and system identifiers
DOCTYPE PUBLIC declarations recover when the public identifier starts immediately after the keyword
DOCTYPE PUBLIC declarations recover when the system identifier starts immediately after the public identifier
DOCTYPE PUBLIC declarations missing a quoted public identifier recover through bogus doctype
DOCTYPE PUBLIC declarations cut off by > inside the public identifier mark force-quirks
DOCTYPE PUBLIC without an identifier marks force-quirks
DOCTYPE SYSTEM declarations preserve the system identifier
DOCTYPE SYSTEM declarations recover when the system identifier starts immediately after the keyword
DOCTYPE SYSTEM declarations missing a quoted system identifier recover through bogus doctype
DOCTYPE SYSTEM declarations cut off by > inside the system identifier mark force-quirks
DOCTYPE SYSTEM without an identifier marks force-quirks
Unexpected text after a system identifier reports a diagnostic without force-quirks
HTML comment opener <!--> emits an empty comment with an abrupt close diagnostic
HTML comment opener <!---> closes as an empty comment before returning to data
Nested-looking <!-- inside a comment remains comment data and reports nested-comment
Comment end bang before > closes the comment with an incorrectly-closed-comment diagnostic
Seeded title RCDATA context emits the matching end tag
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves < into text
Seeded style RAWTEXT context emits the matching end tag while keeping raw text literal
Named character references resolve in data state
Named character references resolve inside quoted and unquoted attribute values
tokens: StartTag(name=a, attributes=[title=Fish & Chips, data-x=<ok>, note="hi"], self_closing=false)

cases.description: Seeded title RCDATA context resolves named character references as text
Legacy named character references resolve in data state
Legacy named character references resolve inside quoted and unquoted attribute values
tokens: StartTag(name=a, attributes=[title=A B, copy=©, reg=®], self_closing=false)

cases.description: Seeded title RCDATA context resolves legacy named character references as text
Classic Latin-1 named character references resolve in data state with case-sensitive names
Classic Latin-1 named character references resolve inside quoted and unquoted attribute values
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves classic Latin-1 named character references as text
WHATWG spacing named character references resolve in data state
WHATWG invisible and non-breaking aliases resolve inside attribute values
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG differential, exponential, and imaginary aliases
WHATWG equality and tilde named character references resolve in data state
WHATWG congruence and equality aliases resolve in data state
WHATWG equality and parallel aliases resolve inside attribute values
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG similarity aliases
WHATWG greater-than and less-than relation aliases resolve inside attribute values
WHATWG greater-than and less-than comparison aliases resolve in data state
WHATWG greater-than and less-than comparison aliases resolve inside attribute values
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG negated greater-than and less-than comparison aliases
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG negated relation aliases, including combining-overlay replacements
WHATWG precedence and successor relation aliases resolve in data state
WHATWG precedence aliases resolve inside attribute values
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG successor aliases
WHATWG basic and double arrow aliases resolve in data state
WHATWG long, bar, tee, and map arrow aliases resolve inside attribute values
WHATWG extended capital, short, diagonal, and double-long arrow aliases resolve in data state
WHATWG lowercase long, hook, tail, two-head, curve, circle, and loop arrow aliases resolve inside attribute values
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG vector and vector-bar arrow aliases
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG harpoon, repeated, negated, squiggle, mapsto, and diagonal arrow aliases
WHATWG Greek variant and letter-like aliases resolve in data state
WHATWG Greek variant aliases resolve inside attribute values
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG Greek variant aliases
WHATWG set and logic aliases resolve in data state
WHATWG membership and subset aliases resolve inside attribute values
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG set operation aliases
WHATWG circled operator, integral, product, and therefore aliases resolve in data state
WHATWG boxed operator and square aliases resolve inside attribute values
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG square, lozenge, star, suit, and symbol aliases
WHATWG box-drawing aliases resolve in data state
WHATWG box-drawing aliases resolve inside attribute values
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG box-drawing aliases
WHATWG angle aliases resolve in data state
WHATWG angle bracket and fence aliases resolve inside attribute values
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG triangle, corner, and over-under fence aliases
WHATWG Latin Extended and diacritic aliases resolve in data state
WHATWG Latin Extended and diacritic aliases resolve inside attribute values
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves WHATWG Latin Extended and diacritic aliases
Legacy named character references recover when the semicolon is missing in data state
Legacy named character references recover when the semicolon is missing in attributes
tokens: StartTag(name=a, attributes=[title=A  B, copy=©, reg=®], self_closing=false)

cases.description: Seeded title RCDATA context recovers legacy named character references with missing semicolons
Missing-semicolon named references only recover through legacy aliases
Named character references resolve known uppercase aliases and unknown references stay literal in data state
Unknown named character references stay literal inside attributes
Decimal and hexadecimal numeric character references resolve in data state
Numeric character references resolve inside quoted and unquoted attribute values
Invalid numeric character references report spec diagnostics and use replacement or remapped characters
Numeric character references without digits stay literal and report absence-of-digits diagnostics
Seeded title RCDATA context resolves numeric character references as text
Numeric character references recover when the semicolon is missing in data state
Numeric character references recover when the semicolon is missing in attributes
Seeded title RCDATA context recovers numeric character references with missing semicolons
Seeded PLAINTEXT context treats markup and character references as literal text
Seeded script data context emits the matching script end tag while keeping character references literal
Seeded script data escaped context emits the matching script end tag
Seeded script data double escaped context keeps the first script end tag literal until the double escape closes
Seeded CDATA section context treats markup and character references as literal text until ]]>, then returns to data
Markup declaration <![CDATA[ enters CDATA section text until ]]>, then returns to data
Processing-instruction-looking markup recovers as a bogus comment
EOF in bogus-comment recovery emits the comment without eof-in-comment
Malformed markup declarations recover as bogus comments with a diagnostic
Malformed empty declarations reconsume > in bogus comment recovery
EOF after a markup declaration opener recovers as an empty bogus comment
Markup declarations with only one opening dash recover as bogus comments
A less-than sign before non-tag text remains text instead of starting a tag
An end-tag opener followed by a non-letter recovers as a bogus comment
WHATWG open-face mathematical alphabet named references decode in data state
WHATWG script mathematical alphabet named references decode in attributes
WHATWG fraktur mathematical alphabet named references decode in RCDATA
WHATWG core Cyrillic named references decode in data state
WHATWG extended Cyrillic named references decode in attributes
WHATWG Cyrillic named references decode in RCDATA
Remaining WHATWG vector named references decode in data state
Remaining WHATWG arrow named references decode in attributes
Remaining WHATWG harpoon named references decode in RCDATA
Remaining WHATWG cap/cup set named references decode in data state
Remaining WHATWG subset/superset named references decode in attributes
Remaining WHATWG negated set named references decode in RCDATA
Remaining WHATWG square-set named references decode in data state
Remaining WHATWG integral named references decode in data state
Remaining WHATWG operator named references decode in attributes
Remaining WHATWG dot operator named references decode in RCDATA
Remaining WHATWG operator-adjacent named references decode in data state
Final WHATWG named character reference coverage batch 1: Assign through angmsdah
Final WHATWG named character reference coverage batch 2: ap through eng
Final WHATWG named character reference coverage batch 3: epar through lcub
Final WHATWG named character reference coverage batch 4: ldca through nsce
Final WHATWG named character reference coverage batch 5: nshortmid through rsh
Final WHATWG named character reference coverage batch 6: rthree through zeetrf
Form feed is an ASCII-whitespace boundary for semicolonless legacy named references
Form feed delimits script double-escape start and end keywords
CRLF and bare CR normalize to LF before HTML tokenization
EOF in an unfinished start tag drops the partial token
EOF in an unfinished ordinary end tag drops the partial token
EOF inside an attribute named character reference drops the partial start tag
EOF inside an attribute numeric character reference drops the partial start tag
